rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Elvia Juare' Renewal Of Judgment, The Memoranudm Of Points And Authorities, And The Statements On The Oath
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, March 18, 2025 Line 1. 3 - DEFENDANT EDWARD NOVAK INDIVIDUALLY AND AS THE EXECUTOR AND ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF RAISSA NOVAK's Motion To Vacate Elvia Juare' Renewal Of Judgment. Defendant Edward Novak's motion to vacate renewal of judgment is denied. Mr. Novak fails to establish a legal basis to vacate the renewal of the judgment. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Plaintiff fails to identify any authority allowing for the requested sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAINIE S. WOOTEN VS. FIVE KEYS SCHOOLS AND PROGRAMS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Rainie S. Wooten'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Five Keys Schools And Programs'S Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, Set One, Numbers 1-77, And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,635.00
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, March 18, 2025 Line 12. 2 - Plaintiff Rainie S. Wooten'S Motion To Compel Defendant Five Keys Schools And Programs'S Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, Set One, Numbers 1-77, And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,635.00. Plaintiff Ranie Wooten's motion to compel further responses to her set one special interrogatories and for monetary sanctions is granted as to the motion to compel and denied as to the monetary sanctions. Defendant Five Keys is required to serve verified code-compliant responses no later than April 18, 2025. The parties are to engage in the Belaire-West process regarding the information of former employees no later than April 4, 2025. With the court of appeal's affirmance of the denial of the motion to compel, there is no longer any actual or arguable judicial economy reason to delay discovery. Although there are two separate cases, the parties are required to ensure that all discovery applies to both cases to avoid duplication and burdening the parties, custodians, third parties, and deponents. The discovery sought by plaintiff meets the standard for discovery relevance and Five Keys has not shown that it would be unduly burdensome to provide that discovery. Because Five Keys acted with substantial justification, no monetary sanctions are awarded. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Five Keys Schools And Programs'S Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Number 1-39, And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $5,835.00
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, March 18, 2025 Line 12. 1 - PLAINTIFF RAINIE WOOTEN's Motion To Compel Defendant Five Keys Schools And Programs'S Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Number 1-39, And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $5,835.00. Plaintiff Ranie Wooten's motion to compel further responses to her set one document requests and for monetary sanctions is granted as to the motion to compel and denied as to the monetary sanctions. Defendant Five Keys is required to serve verified code-compliant responses and produce all non-privileged responsive documents no later than April 18, 2025. Any documents withheld on the grounds of privilege must be included in a privilege log. The parties are to engage in the Belaire-West process regarding the information of former employees no later than April 4, 2025. With the court of appeal's affirmance of the denial of the motion to compel, there is no longer any actual or arguable judicial economy reason to delay discovery. Although there are two separate cases, the parties are required to ensure that all discovery applies to both cases to avoid duplication and burdening the parties, custodians, third parties, and deponents. The discovery sought by plaintiff meets the standard for discovery relevance and Five Keys has not shown that it would be unduly burdensome to provide that discovery. Because Five Keys acted with substantial justification, no monetary sanctions are awarded. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: 1341 UNION STREET TENANCY-IN-COMMON, AN VS. STEVEN SCOTT TOLLEFSRUD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, March 18, 2025 Line 13. 2 - DEFENDANT STEVEN TOLLEFSRUD , INDIVIDUALLY AND AS THE TRUSTEE OF THE STEVEN SCOTT TOLLEFSRUD TRUST DATED DECEMBER 22ND, 1999,MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Specially appearing defendant Scott Hauswirth's motion to quash subpoenas is granted without prejudice to plaintiffs re-serving subpoenas to Coldwell Banker, Ms. Buckles, and First American Title if and when one or more of plaintiffs' claims survive a demurrer. Plaintiffs' claims depend on arguably novel theories of standing and harm. While normally a party may propound discovery requests pending resolution of pleading challenges, the recent demurrer order in this case and the arguable novelty of plaintiffs' theories strongly favor delaying discovery, at least discovery that in whole or in part seeks financial information, until after defendants' pleading challenges have been adjudicated. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, March 18, 2025 Line 7. DEFENDANT SREE VAIDYANATHAN's Amended MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Sree Vaidyanathan's motion to strike the prayer for punitive damages from the first amended complaint is denied. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ZAHIR NASERI VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, March 18, 2025 Line 9. PLAINTIFF ZAHIR NASERI's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Plaintiff Zahir Naseri's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to portions of defendant Uber Technologies' first amended answer is granted as to the fourteenth affirmative defense purporting to reserve the right to allege additional affirmative defenses and denied as to all other portions of the amended answer for which Mr. Naseri seeks judgment in his favor. Uber's fourteenth affirmative defense is invalid. Uber must seek court permission to allege additional affirmative defenses. Uber's general denial and all other affirmative defenses challenged on this motion, including those based on an asserted arbitration agreement, are adequately alleged. Mr. Naseri's declaration was not considered in ruling on this motion since a motion for judgment on the pleadings is a non-evidentiary motion. Mr. Naseri's request for judicial notice is denied because it seeks judicial notice of matters that are not judicially noticeable. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the opposing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DELORES MARTIN VS. MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, March 18, 2025 Line 10. DEFENDANT MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant Mercury Insurance Company's demurrer to both causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Delores Martin is overruled as to both causes of action. The complaint adequately alleges that Mercury Insurance Company is the issuer of the homeowner's insurance policy that is the basis for her claims. Other than the statements regarding meet and confer efforts, Min Kang's declaration and exhibits 5 and 5 thereto filed with the moving papers were not considered in ruling on this demurrer since a motion for judgment on the pleadings is a non-evidentiary motion and the declarant, counsel for defendant and not a party to the insurance policy, lacks personal knowledge about the insurance policy issued to Ms. Martin. Ms. Martin's motion to strike the demurrer is off calendar as moot. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK, N.A. VS. RYAN J WARMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File Counterclaim
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, March 18, 2025 Line 11. DEFENDANT RYAN-JAMES WARMAN , PH.D., APPEARING AS BENEFICIARY, AGENT, AND AUTHORIZED REPRESENTATIVE FOR: RYAN J WARMAN's Motion For Leave To File Counterclaim. Defendant Ryan-James Warman's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint (erroneously referred to in the motion papers as a counterclaim) is granted. Mr. Warman must file and serve his cross-complaint no later than April 2, 2025. California has a strong policy in favor of allowing the filing of pleadings so that cases may be tried on their merits. Plaintiff Citibank has not shown that it would suffer legal prejudice by the granting of this motion. Citibank's arguments regarding the asserted lack of merit to the cross-complaint are better addressed by a pleading motion such as a demurrer or an evidentiary motion such as a motion for summary judgment rather than on a motion to amend, which is particularly true in this case since Mr. Warman's moving papers do not include a copy of his proposed cross-complaint. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.)
